issue_title: NPE if user not exists in PolicyEvaluationRequest
commit_message:
Fix NPE if user not exists

Check "userSession.getId().equals(clientUser.getId())" fails if getUserFromToken return non existed user. It is happens when AccessToken.subject relates to non existed user.

Closes #16297

issue_title: In CI, new-store-integration-tests for CRDB is sometimes cancelled after 70 minutes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

When adding CockroachDB to the integration tests, the build times where around 45-50 minutes. There are now build times around 50-60 minutes, and sometimes the builds have been cancelled



### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

Build should complete

### Actual behavior

Build fails sometimes

### How to Reproduce?

Jan 7, 2023 / [3864297993](https://stianst.github.io/keycloak-dashboard/tests#failed-run-3864297993) / jpa-cockroach / CANCELLED
Jan 6, 2023 / [3858128510](https://stianst.github.io/keycloak-dashboard/tests#failed-run-3858128510) / jpa-cockroach / CANCELLED
Dec 22, 2022 / [3756384206](https://stianst.github.io/keycloak-dashboard/tests#failed-run-3756384206) / jpa-cockroach / CANCELLED

Successful builds: 
https://github.com/ahus1/keycloak/actions/runs/3740105135/jobs/6348195623 (60 min)
https://github.com/ahus1/keycloak/actions/runs/3872075872/jobs/6600686437 (56 min)


### Anything else?

_No response_

Extend build time for CockroachDB (#16315)

Closes #16313

issue_title: EntityField `mapPut` and  `collectionAdd` default methods doesn't insert an element when `get(e)` returns `null`
issue_body: `EntityFiled.get(E e)` may return `null` and in that case default method `mapPut` (and `collectionAdd`) do not insert the element into a map (or into a collection).
commit_message:
EntityField `mapPut` and `collectionAdd` default methods doesn't insert an element when `get(e)` returns `null` 

Closes #16317

issue_title: KcSamlSignedBrokerTest failing in FIPS mode
issue_body:
This is follow-up of #14969 . As even after adding the `XMLDSig` provider to java.security providers, the `KcSamlSignedBrokerTest` is failing when Keycloak server running on FIPS.

Reason is, that BCFIPS does not allow same private key to be used for both signing and decryption, which is actually used in `KcSamlSignedBrokerTest` (affected is private `rsa` key in `consumer` realm. As this realm uses private key for signing IDTokens of it's clients, but at the same time, this private key is used to decrypt encrypted SAML assertions sent from SAML IDP (represented by realm `provider`)

issue_title: Service Accounts Client must create the Client ID mapper with Token Claim Name as client_id
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

[Enabling service account](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_service_accounts) create three default mappers.
One of them is 'Client ID' mapper. This mapper has default 'Token Claim Name' field equal to clientId.
Default 'Token Claim Name' field must be **client_id** in order to be compliant with OAuth2 specifications.
OAuth2 specifications related to **client_id** claim:

- [JSON Web Token (JWT) Profile for OAuth 2.0 Access Tokens ](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9068#section-2.2)
- [OAuth 2.0 Token Introspection](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7662#section-2.2)
- [OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8693#section-4.3)

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

 'Client ID' mapper with default 'Token Claim Name' field equal to client_id.

### Actual behavior

 'Client ID' mapper with default 'Token Claim Name' field equal to clientId.

### How to Reproduce?

[Enable service account](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_service_accounts) in a Client

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Service Accounts Client must create the Client ID mapper with Token Claim Name as client_id

closes #16329

issue_title: Unable to delete realm names with invalid URL characters
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

### Area

### Describe the bug

First I apologize for not being able to test this on the newest version. The version tested was 20.0.1 and I think this is still applicable to the newest release as well. I am not able to perform an installation easy in my work environment so I don't have the newest version to test against. 

During testing, we determined that it was possible to create Realm names that had invalid characters and subsequently couldn't be deleted. The simplest way to reproduce this issue is by doing the following steps:

1.) Create a new realm with the name "%22"

I believe this is because deletions are done using the DELETE method against the realm name within the URL, which won't accept these invalid characters. We would be able to delete the realms created with the incorrect characters

We are unable to delete the realms with the incorrect characters

issue_title: Cannot access subgroups with just "view-users" role except in new tab - javascript error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the new admin theme, if my user has the 'view-users' role of the realm-management client I am able to see and access the "Users" and "Groups" menu items. I can access them and list groups, but I am not able to click on a subgroup due to error "`r().access is undefined`"
I am however able to list the subgroup contents in a new tab by middle-clicking
I can also list the subgroups if I have the "manage-users" role but that is not an option for me since it allows editing user details.

### Version

20.0.0

### Expected behavior

There should be consistency in subgroup listing. If I can list all groups with the "view-users" role, I should be able to view groups as well as subgroups.

### Actual behavior

Subgroups can only be viewed in new tab due to javascript error related to missing "access" key on subgroups.

`r().access is undefined`

### How to Reproduce?

0. Create realm "Bug"
1. Create a parent group and a child group:
  * Parent
      * Child
2. Create a user and add the 'view-users' role of the realm-management client to it using the role mapping page -> client-roles -> realm-management
3. Log in with the new user in the realm console
4. Click "Groups" - Groups will appear
5. Click the "Parent" group - subgroups will apear
6. Click the "Child" group - you will see error - `r().access is undefined`

### Anything else?

I have investigate requests and it seems when listing groups the first request done is:
`realms/Bug/admin-ui-groups?search=&first=0&max=101&global=false`
This returns a list of groups containing keys including "`subgroups`" and "`access`"
In our case, since we only have the 'view-users' role, the access key contains the following:
~~~ json
{
	"access": {
		"view": true,
		"manage": false,
		"manageMembership": false
	}
}
~~~
However the child group contains no such key:
~~~ json
{
	"0": {
		"id": "245c20d7-5728-4ce3-bfdf-3ddd7b7ac4cf",
		"name": "Child",
		"path": "/Parent/Child",
		"attributes": {},
		"realmRoles": [],
		"clientRoles": {},
		"subGroups": []
	}
}
~~~

Now the weird thing is, if I give the user the role "`manage-users`", the access key of the parent becomes:
~~~ json
{
	"access": {
		"view": true,
		"manage": true,
		"manageMembership": true
	}
}
~~~
And it allows clicking on the Child group although this child still doesn't have the "access" key.
I think `"manage": true` circumvents that bug but it isn't an option for me.

It's also not an option to use older theme, since it lacks the functionality to add users into groups in the group listing entirely.

## Background - fyi
My use case is the following: I want to have users be able to see and browse through groups without being able to edit user details. I will use this functionality with fine-grained permissions in order to have team leaders manage membership of their own groups without letting them edit their users, which is something HR will manage. Setup works. I can add users to subgroups in the users page, but I cannot access the subgroup to add users into it except using "open in new tab"

add fine grain access to subgroups (#16672)

issue_title: Documentation - Expand/Clarify Admin REST API User Search Functionality
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

The documentation around queries for user information via the Admin REST API is incomplete/misleading.

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

Clear explanations of the behaviors and options available given how the system is configured.

### Actual behavior

Documentation does not present a whole/accurate picture of behaviors/options.

### How to Reproduce?

When an AD/LDAP federation is configured and the sync option is enabled user information is queried and cached in the Keycloak database.  After this it appears that on every query of the Admin API for users (ex. GET /{realm}/users) the result is Keycloak issuing an LDAP query *per user* for user attributes, etc.  If the system has many thousands of users this is not desirable behavior.
On the other hand when an AD/LDAP federation is configured and the sync option is *disabled* the user information is never pulled down from AD to the Keycloak database.  In this case when the same query is run against the API no results (or only users created locally) are returned. However the addition of "?username=*" results in *one* LDAP query (in my case for sAMAccountName=*) with the appropriate...based on the DNs and other options configured in the federation setup...result set.  However the use of the wildcard/asterisk is not documented *and* it does not seem to work if the sync option is enabled.

### Anything else?

Keycloak documentation states "The advantage of synchronization is that all Keycloak features work efficiently because any required extra per-user data is stored locally." [link](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#storage-mode).  However issuing thousands of queries to an AD/LDAP server does not seem to be an efficient method of querying when the scope of the query is fairly large.  Providing expanded documentation in this space might allow users to better understand the ramifications of their configuration choices and how to leverage the options (like the wildcard search in an system not sync'ing) available to them.

issue_title: Token introspection response does not follow RFC6479 "scope" parameter format
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

Currently, the introspection response include a scope parameter even if no scope is granted. It will be empty, e.g. `"scope": ""`.

Token introspection spec in [RFC6479 section 3.3](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6749#section-3.3) specifies the format of this field is:

     scope       = scope-token *( SP scope-token )
     scope-token = 1*( %x21 / %x23-5B / %x5D-7E )

My understanding is that means "at least one scope-token" and scope-token must include "at least one character" (in those ranges).

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

If no scope is granted, then the "scope" field should not be present.

### Actual behavior

An empty value of `"scope": ""` is included.

### How to Reproduce?

Request a grant with no scopes, and call the token introspection endpoint.

### Anything else?

This behavior is the same as in access tokens per #16523 , however I consider that a separate issue because Keycloak defines its own format of JWTs for access tokens, and this is not specified by OAuth. So  16523 is more of a "nice to have" to be consistent with token introspection (as well as RFC9068).

This can be worked around by requesting at least one scope.
commit_message:
Do not return empty scope field in token introspection response

Closes #16526

Fix pagination issue with H6

With Hibernate ORM 6, pagination started to be unreliable: When
setting the max results only if the first row was 0 has randomly
affected other threads where first row was greater than 0. The
latter thread sometimes produced query which did *not* account
for the offset (cf. threads `-t1` and `-t2` below, while `-t2`
missed the `offset ? rows` part whic `-t3` has).

This has been fixed by setting the first row offset unconditionally.

Closes: #20202
Closes: #16570

issue_title: Setting user password and entering "password confirmation" first leads to blocking of "save"
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

Try setting a password for a user and enter the "password confirmation" field first and then "password" afterwards. Even if both password are exactly the same the app says "Password and confirmation does not match." and won't let you save.

Sure it is not the expected workflow to first fill "password confirmation" but in my case a made a typo in the first password field and I went back to correct it just to see that the app doesn't care for my correction.

Please make sure that BOTH fields get the necessary validation listeners assigned to.

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

Regardless of the order of input both password fields should be checked for match.

### Actual behavior

Only on inputing into the "password confirmation" field leads to check for match.

### How to Reproduce?

Type in the "password confirmation" first and then in the "password" field.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Impossible to update a federated user credential label
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

When an administrator modifies a federated user credential's label from the admin UI and click on "Save", the new user label is not saved.

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

When modifying a federated user credential's label from the admin UI and click on "Save", the new user label is saved.

### Actual behavior

When modifying a federated user credential's label from the admin UI and click on "Save", the new user label is not saved.

### How to Reproduce?

1) Create a federated user with a credential
2) In the admin UI, search for this user
3) In the credentials tab, modify the credential label and click on save

### Anything else?

The bug comes from org.keycloak.storage.jpa.JpaUserFederatedStorageProvider#updateCredential(org.keycloak.models.RealmModel, java.lang.String, org.keycloak.credential.CredentialModel) : the entity is not updated with the user label coming from the credential model given in parameter, it is the other way around

Impossible to update a federated user credential label
Closes #16613

issue_title: Snyk workflow failing when running the checks against the Operator
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

ci

### Describe the bug

The Snyk analysis when running to check the Operator is failing since last month. It seems that the build command is unable to retrieve additional dependencies:

```
Failed to get dependencies for all 1 potential projects.
Tip: Re-run in debug mode to see more information: DEBUG=*snyk* <COMMAND>
If the issue persists contact support@snyk.io
```

Later on, the workflow fails because the SARIF file was not generated after the build:

```
Run github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v2.1.37
Error: Path does not exist: operator-report.sarif
Error: Path does not exist: operator-report.sarif

    at async runWrapper (/home/runner/work/_actions/github/codeql-action/v2.1.37/lib/upload-sarif-action.js:68:9)
```

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

- Snyk workflow to pass

### Actual behavior

- Snyk workflow failing

### How to Reproduce?

- See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/workflows/snyk-analysis.yml

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Database migrations are not transactional
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Hey there!

It appears that migrations in Keycloak are not done in a transactional manner - that is, if you launch two or more Keycloaks on an empty database, there is a good chance that one of them will crash because it will try to upgrade the database schema while the first instance is still initializing. This came as a surprise to me - while schema modifications are not transactional in quite a few databases, the usual schema migration tools guard against this by using a "lock table" (e.g. DATABASECHANGELOGLOCK for Liquibase), so I assume this is unintentional.

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should start - one instance should be doing the schema upgrade, the other instance should block trying to acquire a lock while that is in progress.

### Actual behavior

Specifically, note that
```
keycloak_1  | 2023-01-26 12:23:19,428 INFO  [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.storage.legacy.liquibase.QuarkusJpaUpdaterProvider] (main) Initializing database schema. Using changelog META-INF/jpa-changelog-master.xml

```
which for me looks like the code in `QuarkusJpaUpdaterProvider` is actually not guarded against running concurrently in different instances.

### How to Reproduce?

The easiest way to reproduce this is using docker-compose:
```
mkdir conf
keytool -genkeypair -storepass password -storetype PKCS12 -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -dname "CN=server" -alias server -ext "SAN:c=DNS:localhost,IP:127.0.0.1" -keystore conf/server.keystore
```
Create a file named `docker-compose.yaml` with the following contents:
```
version: '3'

services:
  database:
    container_name: keycloak_db
    image: "postgres:14"
    command: [ "postgres"]
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5433:5432"
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: "keycloak"
      POSTGRES_USER: "postgres"
```

issue_title: BCFIPS approved mode: Some tests failing due the short secret for client-secret-jwt client authentication
issue_body:
Some tests are failing in BCFIPS approved mode due the SecretKey used for `HmacSHA256` (or others of HmacSHA algorithms) should be at least 112 bits long (14 bytes).

This means that for clients using `client-secret-jwt` authentication, the client secrets must be at least 14 characters long. This is an issue in some of our tests (at least ClientAuthSecretSignedJWTTest, FAPI1Test, KcOidcBrokerClientSecretJwtTest), where hardcoded secrets were shorter.

This won't be an issue in the typical deployments due the client secrets are usually generated, but we still need to document this as it is possible to use hardcoded client secrets in Keycloak (EG. via realm import).
commit_message:
BCFIPS approved mode: Some tests failing due the short secret for client-secret-jwt client authentication
Closes #16678

issue_title: Fix remaining failing tests with BCFIPS approved mode
issue_body:
There are still few failing tests with BCFIPS approved mode. Those are:
- Few tests failing because of using 1024 long RSA key (not allowed in BCFIPS approved mode)
- Few tests dependent on Kerberos/SPNEGO available (ResetPasswordTest, JsonFileImport198MigrationTest), which is not true with FIPS where Kerberos feature is disabled by default
- Few tests failing because of `keystore.type.compat` security feature was explicitly set to `false`, which makes it impossible to read default java truststore from cacerts file

issue_title: CVE-2022-45047 - wildfly-elytron: Deserialization of Untrusted Data vulnerability in org.apache.sshd:sshd-common
issue_body:
# Overview

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Deserialization of Untrusted Data via `org.apache.sshd.server.keyprovider.SimpleGeneratorHostKeyProvider`.

# Details

Serialization is a process of converting an object into a sequence of bytes which can be persisted to a disk or database or can be sent through streams. The reverse process of creating object from sequence of bytes is called deserialization. Serialization is commonly used for communication (sharing objects between multiple hosts) and persistence (store the object state in a file or a database). It is an integral part of popular protocols like _Remote Method Invocation (RMI)_, _Java Management Extension (JMX)_, _Java Messaging System (JMS)_, _Action Message Format (AMF)_, _Java Server Faces (JSF) ViewState_, etc.

_Deserialization of untrusted data_ ([CWE-502](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/502.html)) is when the application deserializes untrusted data without sufficiently verifying that the resulting data will be valid, thus allowing the attacker to control the state or the flow of the execution.

# Remediation

Upgrade WildFly Elytron to version 1.20.3.CR1 or higher.

- https://issues.redhat.com/browse/ELY-2492
- Comments from https://github.com/wildfly-security/wildfly-elytron/pull/1846:

"_The affected class (org.apache.sshd.server.keyprovider.SimpleGeneratorHostKeyProvider) isn't used by WildFly Elytron so WildFly Elytron isn't affected by https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-fhw8-8j55-vwgq_"

# References

-   [Apache Archive](https://www.mail-archive.com/dev@mina.apache.org/msg39312.html)
-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/apache/mina-sshd/commit/5a8fe830b2a2308a2b24ac8115a391af477f64f5)

issue_title: Make sure PBKDF2 providers are using the expect size for derived keys
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

The `pbkdf2-sha256` provider is using derived keys with size `512`, increasing the time taken to hash passwords. When using this provider, derived keys should be `256`.

Using the `CryptoTest` to evaluate the times to compute hashes, the `pbkdf2-sha256` provider is doubling the time because it is using the wrong dkLen.

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

Derived key size matching the algorithms used for hashing passwords.

### Actual behavior

The `pbkdf2-sha256` provider is using derived keys with size `512`, increasing the time taken to hash passwords. When using this provider, derived keys should be `256`.

### How to Reproduce?

Evaluate the time taken to hash user passwords.

_No response_
